creative_brief: Photo-style image: a real moment at the Brookvale hub where a farmer and a customer are talking over a counter with recognisable produce (pork cuts, eggs). Warm lighting, no text overlay. Community feel, not retail feel.
primary_text:
When Rachel Ward started supplying us from her farm in Kempsey, something shifted.

Families collecting from our Brookvale hub started talking to each other. Swapping recipes. Asking which farm grew the carrots their kids actually ate.

Then they started asking a different question: "How do we become part of this?"

Not as customers. As co-owners.

Because when you know your eggs come from Farmer Brown and your salmon comes wild-caught off the South Coast, you stop feeling like a transaction. You feel like you belong to something.

No middlemen. No wholesalers. Just a community that owns what it eats.

We can't promise where this goes. But we can show you where your food comes from.
headline: They didn't want to shop here. They wanted to own it.
description: Be part of a farm-direct grocery store built by the people who eat from it.
